reviews.review_description: I'm not sure how this game manages to get such a good rating when it's the most "yeah, that'll do" game ever. There's so little effort put into literally every aspect. What's the story? There isn't one. Not at all. The gameplay consists of being dropped into one of a handful of maps that constantly repeat and being given a basic objective like "destroy the (suspiciously modern looking for a WW2 game) generators" or "destroy the MG-9 nests" (once you've dealt with the MG-9 operators you can't even use the guns yourself; how lazy is that?) Somehow the sniper and tank modes are even worse. The graphics are like a bargain bin PS2 game and the dreadful cutscenes are shown on a flat screen like the laziest VR conversion. I also had times where the game refused to quit to the point that others apps would run with this still going on the background. There's nothing worthwhile in the game and, worst of all, it's really boring.

developer_response.body:
Hi,

We have released a new update based on the issues you mentioned. The timeline/cutscene issue has now been fixed, so scenes are no longer shown on plane textures. The modern looking generator has also been replaced with a proper World War II generator model.

This update also includes:

Players can now pick up enemy weapons.
Enemy weapons have been replaced with more realistic models.
Realistic enemy death reactions with improved ragdoll physics have been added.
Enemy helmets now drop on death for a more immersive experience.
Minor bugs have been fixed and overall stability has been improved.

We  understand there are still areas that need improvement, including story, mission variety, and gameplay depth. These points are already planned for upcoming updates.

Please update to the latest version and try the game again. If these fixes improve your experience, I kindly request you to update your rating and review, as it really helps support the continued development of the game.

Thank you again for your detailed feedback.

reviews.review_title: Where Above and Beyond should have aimed
reviews.review_description:
Not long ago, I bought a Quest 3 to play the final Medal of Honor game* and I bought this so I could see what a WWII shooter should feel like baseline while Above and Beyond was installing.

I Medal of Honor at its core is paying respects to the veterans of the Congressional Medal of Honor society, but the game's fallen into the trap of "Oh no, evil Nazis killing people for no reason" and feels more like MachineGame's Wolfenstein (no offence, still very enjoyable).

This clearly takes inspiration from Allied Assault and plays like a combination of Medal of Honor Allied Assault and Call of Duty 2, it's classic, runs well and looks good on the Quest 3S and has that old "just as soon as I grab these documents" feel (SPOILER: You end up doing way more than that).

However, with that said, I do have some suggestions that would potentially improve immersion.

1. Serialise the missions - I get the appeal of different environments but I think it would be cool to start off doing the Stalingrad missions first, then off into Tunisia, and having the Normandy missions last, connecting them with some sort of vague story like how the soldier and his dog are in a Secret Service operating behind enemy lines.

2. Sparingly use paratroopers - The amount of Fallschirmjäger is pretty hilarious, but doesn't make sense in some spots. Maybe introduce ambushes to catch players off guard instead?

3. Available weaponry - One of the selling points seems to be the use of authentic weapons. However, my only issue is that you can use Soviet weaponry in Africa? I would make it so that weapons that made an appearance in the battle are free/cheaper as a "you-can-purchase-for-this-theatre" and make it cost more points if you want it permanently or to lock certain weapons out of certain theatres (Side note: I see an Arisaka, are we getting Pacific missions?).

Overall, this game is really fun and captures the spirit of classic World War 2 shooters and at a really good price!

Easy game to get into with short entertaining missions, i assume comes from the game being a port of a mobile game. I would like to see fast smooth turn option and a crouch button as there is only click turning at the moment. The newly introduced Sniper mode has the great bullet follow mechanic after firing like Zombie Army but there is not much impact when hitting target, could do with some nice slow motion blood splatter. The dog is a fun edition, I would like to be able to pet him and give a pat after taking down enemies. Early Access game so improving all the time.

This game has some bugs. I only played 7 missions so far. 

The Thompson can’t reload on realistic mode but turning it off is still tons of fun.

AI is brain dead even on hard mode.

Game is closest to getting the original Medal of Honor in VR . Graphics look just like it . But levels are way too short some are a few minutes and if they put 20% more effort on making a story it may be worth $10.

But important thing it’s fun to play and it’s only $5. There are worse WW2 shooters in Vr.

Edit: the developers responded and updated the game. The realistic reload works perfect now. On hard mode it’s a bit harder. Game is even  better now

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for taking the time to play the game, share detailed feedback, and update your review after our fixes, we truly appreciate your support.

We’re really glad to hear that the realistic reload issue is now working well for you and that the experience feels better on hard mode. Feedback like yours helps us improve the gameplay, AI balance, and overall polish.

Since your last play session, we have also released a major update that introduces a brand new Tank Mode, so the game now features three different gameplay modes: Soldier, Sniper, and Tank for the same $4.99 price. We are continuing to work on longer missions, improved AI behavior, visual upgrades, and more story content in future updates.

Thank you again for supporting the game, it means a lot to our team. We hope you continue enjoying the updates

reviews.review_description:
I was surprised how great is this game.
It is an extremely low cost, hidden gem.
The game is like a VR version of the first CoD and MoH games... graphically and in the gameplay.
My only complaint, is the same as some friends: very few enemies... I was hoping to confront and engage an overwhelming nazi force. 
For the rest, looks like a in development game, but it looks definitively a very, very promising game.
Totally recommended.
Get it while still in discount.

Ok after the newest update I feel this has definitely gotten better. The enemy AI still isn't the greatest but better and I love the sniper modes bullet cam. However the sniper rifle scope is a bit awkward to use and I just resorted to aiming without it and doing much better. Also after sniper mission 2 I was kicked back to the main menu? 

Anyways its much improved and with continued work I'd say its worthy of 4 stars

Original review:

This is a pretty good start for a game but it has some bugs. (Guns I've bought revert back to un-purchased after missions randomly, enemies walk and fire through walls, ect.) Also the enemy AI is about as smart as rock. They barely take cover, mostly just sticking to running straight at you. Missions are short, even on hard i dont think any of the missions (I'm up to mission 10) have taken more than 2 minutes to complete. Theres some decent level designs but the world is pretty empty until you hit a checkpoint and 3 or 4 enemies appear ready for slaughter. And what's up with the death match announcer yelling out your headshots? Theres not even a multi-player mode so why the announcer?? 

With some more work put into it, this could become a good game but unfortunately as it stands now it feels more like a very early Alpha version of a game that needed a good bit more development time.
So fun if you got drafted into a war and you played this you probably be good at war

reviews.review_title: This is a fantastic little game. What a
reviews.review_description:
Not sure why people are saying it’s buggy I played for a whole half hour and I love it. I honestly would’ve expected. I have a lot more price for this type of game. The flow is excellent. The mechanics are super and it’s just a plain out fun game to play.
Great job Dev

reviews.review_title: Intense and immersive WW2 VR shooter
reviews.review_description: Call of Courage VR delivers exactly what I was looking for in a World War II VR shooter. The gunplay feels solid, the environments are immersive, and the missions keep the tension high. Handling weapons in VR feels natural, especially the rifles. If you enjoy realistic combat and story driven missions, this is worth playing.

It’s quite enjoyable, but it has room for improvement. I understand that it’s a VR port of the mobile game, but the graphics are subpar, and there are some bugs. However, the gameplay isn’t particularly engaging because some of the elements are repetitive and become monotonous. Nevertheless, it’s a promising start. The only way to enhance it is to remove all AI, as it’s generally disliked and considered terrible.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback and for trying the game.

Since your review, we have released major updates where we added two new gameplay modes: Sniper Mode and Tank Mode. We have also fixed several bugs, improved gameplay polish, and increased overall graphic quality.

We are continuously working to make the experience better. If you get a chance to try the latest update and enjoy the improvements, kindly consider updating your review. Your support really helps us improve the game.

Thank you!

reviews.review_description: This game surprised me in a good way. The atmosphere, sound design, and enemy encounters make you feel like you’re actually in a WW2 battlefield. Performance is smooth, and the controls work well on Quest. You can tell a lot of effort went into this project.
Had some fun but there isn't enough to it and 5$ is a bit of a scam

developer_response.body:
Thank you for trying the game and for sharing your feedback.

We have recently released a major update where we added a completely new Tank Mode, so now the game includes three different gameplay modes: Soldier, Sniper, and Tank for the same $4.99 price.

We are continuously improving the game with new content and updates. If you get a chance to try the new Tank Mode and enjoy the improvements, we would really appreciate if you consider updating your rating.

Your feedback helps us make the game better. Thank you for your support!

reviews.review_description: This game offers a good mix of action and realism. Weapon handling feels natural, enemies are challenging, and the level design keeps things interesting. You can tell the developer cares about realism and immersion.
Love the old moh music, needs longer missions
Experience the actual World War game. Great work.👍

Feels very old sorry, was hoping it was multiplayer , body weapons get in way, are you doing a remake , graphics could be better,  still i paid 2 pound bought 3 copys for gifts . Now i kinda embarrassed , oncecagain threw money away , sorry old boys butvtally no eh chaps

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for supporting the game and for even buying extra copies as gifts, we truly appreciate it.

We recently released a major update that adds a completely new Tank Mode, and now the game includes three different gameplay modes: Soldier, Sniper, and Tank for the same price. We are also actively working on further improvements, including gameplay polish, visual upgrades, and new content updates.

Your feedback about graphics, weapon handling, and multiplayer expectations is very valuable to us and helps guide future updates. If you get a chance to try the new Tank Mode and the latest improvements, we would be grateful if you consider updating your review.

Thank you again for your support and honesty, it really means a lot to our small development team.

reviews.review_description:
loved the way the game reacts fast, was fun to play.
can't wait for more new levels.
